items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 26 Jun: Greenback ends mixed, still higher for week
items.summary: Major stock indices close modestly lower with the Nasdaq leading the way. Rubio: Framework raises hopes for peace, but major obstacles remain Crude oil settles the day/week at $69.23 Hezbollah's Fadlallah: Cannot enforce Washington agreement unless they go to civil war US, Israel and Lebanon sign trilateral agreement Trump says any European country that implements digital services tax will get 100% tariff Bitcoin threatening to close below the 200 week MA for the first time since October 2023 The Empire Strikes Back is the theme in today's market Fed's Kashkari: May need to raise rates amid broad inflation UMich final June consumer sentiment 49.5 vs 50.0 expected The market is souring on the idea of AI at all costs. Five thoughts on what's coming next US May wholesale inventories +0.3% vs +0.3% expected US May advance goods trade balance -105.8 billion vs -85.0 billion expected The USD is mixed with some late week dollar selling seen in the major currency pairs investingLive European markets wrap: Oil drops further, stocks down with eyes on big tech The U.S. dollar ended Friday slightly mixed but still posted a gain for the week as investors turned more cautious toward risk assets. The greenback came under pressure from falling U.S. Treasury yields, particularly at the short and intermediate maturities, and another sharp decline in crude oil prices as markets grew increasingly confident that Middle East tensions would not disrupt energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz. On the currency front, the dollar fell vs the EUR, GBP, CAD but declines were modest. The greenback rose vs the AUD and NZD. It as near unchanged vs the CHF and JPY. Dollar Performance vs. Major Currencies EUR/USD: Euro rose 0.13% to 1.1384 GBP/USD: British pound rose 0.01% to 1.3192 USD/JPY: Dollar fell 0.02% to 161.74 yen USD/CHF: Dollar fell 0.05% to 0.8094 Swiss francs USD/CAD: Canadian dollar rose 0.09%, with USD/CAD falling to 1.4185 AUD/USD: Australian dollar fell 0.22% to 0.6893 NZD/USD: New Zealand dollar fell 0.19% to 0.5636 For the trading week, the USD ended stronger vs all the major currencies with the largest gains coming vs the AUD and the NZD. The greenbacks gains were limited vs the JPY as the USDJPY tested the 40 year high at 161.95 and found willing sellers. EUR, +0.72% JPY +0.27% GBP +0.31% CHF +0.33% CAD, +0.27% AUD +1.70% NZD +1.82% The broader S&P and NASDAQ indices closed lower for the fifth consecutive days (althoug declines in the S&P were modest over the last three trading days). The NASDAQ was the weakest performer, falling 0.24%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.09% and the S&P 500 edged lower by 0.05%. The NASDAQ index was down 4.60% for the week.The S&P 500 was down 1.95%, its worst weekly performance since the week ending June 1.The Dow Jones Industrial Average continues to hold up relatively well. Although it slipped 0.09% on the day, the blue-chip index still gained 0.60% for the week and remains higher by 1.65% for June. US yields today fell in the shorter and. The longer end saw modest gains: 2 year yield 4.096%, -2.4 basis points 5 year yield 4.133%, -2.9 basis points 10 year yield 4.376% -1.5 basis points 30 year yield 4.868%, +0.9 point basis points Looking at the economic data today: US advance goods trade balance The goods deficit widened sharply to -$105.8B versus -$85.0B expected and -$82.4B prior. Exports fell while imports rose, making net trade a likely drag on Q2 GDP trackers. Bias: weaker. US wholesale/retail inventories Wholesale inventories rose 0.3%, in line with estimates, while retail inventories rose 0.6%. Inventory accumulation can support GDP, but only if demand holds up. Bias: neutral to mildly stronger. University of Michigan consumer sentiment Final June sentiment came in at 49.5, below the 50.0 expected, but above the preliminary 48.9 and May’s 44.8. Consumers are still gloomy, but sentiment improved from May. Bias: mixed, but still weak overall. Overall bias The combined data lean weaker economically. The larger trade deficit is the main negative, sentiment remains depressed, and inventories provide only a modest offset. Fed's Kashkari spoke for the first time since the FOMC rate decision last week. Kashkari struck a notably hawkish tone on Friday, saying he has shifted from expecting a rate cut earlier this year to now penciling in one rate hike by year-end. Kashkari said inflation pressures are becoming more broad-based and are no longer just about higher energy prices tied to the Middle East conflict. He also expressed concern that geopolitical risks remain elevated and cautioned against assuming the inflation threat has passed. Kashkari emphasized that his forecast is not set in stone—calling it "a pencil" projection that will depend on incoming data—but reiterated that inflation has been running too high for too long and remains the Fed's top priority. His comments reinforce the increasingly hawkish shift within the Fed, where a growing number of policymakers now see the possibility that rates may need to move higher rather than lower if inflation fails to cool. Next week the US jobs report will highlight the week's data. The date will be released on Thursday due to the observance of the July 4th holiday on July 3. Fed Chair Warsh and other central bankers will highlight the speakers when they all speak on Wednesday at 9 AM. Key Economic Calendar: Week of June 29 – July 3 Monday, June 29 Key Speakers 1:30 PM ET: ECB President Christine Lagarde speaks. Tuesday, June 30 Economic Releases German Preliminary CPI (m/m): Forecast +0.1% | Prior -0.2% Canada GDP (m/m): Forecast +0.4% | Prior -0.1% U.S. Conference Board Consumer Confidence: Forecast 94.2 | Prior 93.1 U.S. JOLTS Job Openings: Forecast 7.28M | Prior 7.62M Wednesday, July 1 Economic Releases Eurozone Core CPI Flash Estimate (y/y): Forecast 2.5% | Prior 2.5% Eurozone CPI Flash Estimate (y/y): Forecast 3.0% | Prior 3.2% U.S. ADP Employment Change: Forecast +118K | Prior +122K U.S. ISM Manufacturing PMI: Forecast 53.7 | Prior 54.0 U.S. ISM Manufacturing Prices Paid: Forecast 79.0 | Prior 82.1 Key Speakers 9:00 AM ET: BOC Governor Tiff Macklem speaks. 9:00 AM ET: ECB President Christine Lagarde speaks. 9:00 AM ET: BOE Governor Andrew Bailey speaks. 9:00 AM ET: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh speaks. 10:30 AM ET: ECB President Christine Lagarde speaks. Thursday, July 2 Economic Releases Switzerland CPI (m/m): Forecast +0.1% | Prior +0.2% U.S. Average Hourly Earnings (m/m): Forecast +0.3% | Prior +0.3% U.S. Non-Farm Payrolls: Forecast +114K | Prior +172K U.S. Unemployment Rate: Forecast 4.3% | Prior 4.3% U.S. Initial Jobless Claims: Forecast 220K | Prior 215K Friday, July 3 Key Speakers 4:00 AM ET: ECB President Christine Lagarde speaks. 11:00 AM ET: BOE Governor Andrew Bailey speaks This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 主要股指小幅收低，纳斯达克指数领跌。鲁比奥：框架协议为和平带来希望，但仍面临重大障碍。原油日收价及周收价定格在69.23美元。真主党法德拉拉表示： 除非爆发内战，否则无法执行华盛顿协议 美国、以色列和黎巴嫩签署三方协议 特朗普表示，任何实施数字服务税的欧洲国家都将面临100%的关税 比特币面临自2023年10月以来首次收于200周移动平均线下方的风险 “帝国反击战”成为今日市场的主旋律 美联储卡什卡里： 在通胀普遍加剧的情况下，可能需要加息 密歇根大学6月最终消费者信心指数为49.5，低于预期的50.0 市场对“不惜一切代价发展AI”的观念日益冷淡。关于接下来走势的五点思考：美国5月批发库存环比增长0.3%，符合预期；美国5月商品贸易顺差初值为-1058亿美元，低于预期值-850亿美元；美元走势涨跌互现，主要货币对中出现周末尾盘抛售；investingLive欧洲市场综述：油价进一步下跌，股市走低，市场关注科技巨头；美国 上周五，美元走势略显震荡，但本周仍录得上涨，因投资者对风险资产的态度趋于谨慎。受美国国债收益率（尤其是短期和中期品种）走低以及原油价格再度大幅下跌的影响，美元承压；市场越来越确信，中东局势紧张不会扰乱霍尔木兹海峡的能源供应。在汇市方面，美元兑欧元、英镑和加元走低，但跌幅有限。美元兑澳元和新西兰元走高。兑瑞士法郎和日元则基本持平。美元对主要货币表现 欧元/美元：欧元上涨0.13%，报1.1384 英镑/美元：英镑上涨0.01%，报1.3192 美元/日元：美元下跌0.02%，报161.74日元 美元/瑞士法郎：美元下跌0.05%，报0.8094瑞士法郎 美元/加元： 加元上涨0.09%，美元兑加元汇率跌至1.4185 澳元兑美元：澳元下跌0.22%，报0.6893 新西兰元兑美元：新西兰元下跌0.19%，报0.5636 本周交易中，美元兑所有主要货币均走强，其中兑澳元和新西兰元的涨幅最大。 美元兑日元涨幅有限，因美元兑日元汇率在触及161.95这一40年高点时遭遇了大量抛售。欧元，+0.72% 日元 +0.27% 英镑 +0.31% 瑞士法郎 +0.33% 加元，+0.27% 澳元 +1.70% 新西兰元 +1.82% 标普500指数和纳斯达克指数连续第五个交易日收低（尽管标普500指数在过去三个交易日的跌幅较为温和）。纳斯达克指数表现最弱，下跌0.24%，而道琼斯工业平均指数微跌0.09%，标普500指数小幅下挫0.05%。纳斯达克指数本周累计下跌4.60%。标普500指数下跌1.95%，创下自6月1日当周以来的最差周表现。道琼斯工业平均指数表现依然相对稳健。尽管当日微跌0.09%，但该蓝筹股指数本周仍上涨0.60%，6月以来累计涨幅仍达1.65%。今日美国短期和长期国债收益率均出现下跌。 长期债券收益率小幅上升：2年期收益率为4.096%，下降2.4个基点；5年期收益率为4.133%，下降2.9个基点；10年期收益率为4.376%，下降1.5个基点；30年期收益率为4.868%，上升0.9个基点。来看今天的经济数据： 美国商品贸易平衡初步数据：商品贸易逆差大幅扩大至-1058亿美元，而市场预期为-850亿美元，前值为-824亿美元。出口下降而进口上升，这可能使贸易净额成为第二季度GDP指标的拖累因素。市场预期：偏弱。美国批发/零售库存：批发库存上升0.3%，符合预期；零售库存上升0.6%。库存积累可支撑GDP，但前提是需求能够保持稳定。倾向：中性至略微偏强。密歇根大学消费者信心指数 6月终值为49.5，低于预期的50.0，但高于初值48.9和5月的44.8。消费者情绪依然低迷，但较5月有所改善。倾向：喜忧参半，但整体仍显疲软。总体倾向：综合来看，这些数据显示经济走势偏弱。贸易逆差扩大是主要利空因素，市场情绪依然低迷，而库存仅起到微弱的对冲作用。美联储的卡什卡里自上周FOMC利率决议以来首次发表讲话。 卡什卡里周五发表了明显偏鹰派的言论，称他已从今年早些时候预期降息，转变为目前预计年底前将加息一次。卡什卡里表示，通胀压力正变得更加广泛，不再仅仅是与中东冲突相关的能源价格上涨所致。他还对地缘政治风险依然居高不下表示担忧，并告诫不要以为通胀威胁已经过去。卡什卡里强调，他的预测并非一成不变——称其为“初步”预测，将取决于后续数据——但他重申，通胀水平过高且持续时间过长，仍是美联储的首要任务。他的言论印证了美联储内部日益鹰派的转向，越来越多的决策者现在认为，如果通胀未能降温，利率可能需要上调而非下调。下周，美国就业报告将成为当周数据的重头戏。由于7月3日适逢7月4日国庆节假期，该报告将于周四发布。美联储主席沃什及其他央行官员将于周三上午9点发表讲话，届时他们将成为关注焦点。 重要经济日历：6月29日–7月3日当周 6月29日（周一） 重要发言人 美国东部时间下午1:30：欧洲央行行长克里斯蒂娜·拉加德发表讲话。 6月30日（周二） 经济数据发布 德国初步CPI（环比）：预期 +0.1% | 前值 -0.2% 加拿大GDP（环比）： 预期 +0.4% | 前值 -0.1% 美国经济咨询局消费者信心指数：预期 94.2 | 前值 93.1 美国JOLTS职位空缺数据：预期 728万 | 前值 762万 7月1日（周三） 经济数据发布 欧元区核心CPI初值（同比）： 预期 2.5% | 前值 2.5% 欧元区CPI初值（同比）：预期 3.0% | 前值 3.2% 美国ADP就业人数变动：预期 +11.8万 | 前值 +12.2万 美国ISM制造业PMI：预期 53.7 | 前值 54.0 美国ISM制造业采购价格指数： 预期 79.0 | 前值 82.1 重要演讲 美国东部时间上午9:00：加拿大央行行长蒂夫·麦克莱姆发表讲话。 美国东部时间上午9:00：欧洲央行行长克里斯蒂娜·拉加德发表讲话。 美国东部时间上午9:00：英国央行行长安德鲁·贝利发表讲话。 美国东部时间上午9:00：美联储主席凯文·沃什发表讲话。 美国东部时间上午10:30：欧洲央行行长克里斯蒂娜·拉加德发表讲话。7月2日（星期四）经济数据发布 瑞士CPI（环比）：预测 +0.1% | 前期 +0.2% 美国平均时薪（环比）：预测 +0.3% | 前期 +0.3% 美国非农就业人数：预测 +11.4万 | 前期 +17.2万 美国 失业率：预期 4.3% | 前值 4.3% 美国初请失业金人数：预期 220K | 前值 215K 7月3日（星期五）重要发言人 美东时间上午4:00：欧洲央行行长克里斯蒂娜·拉加德发表讲话。 美东时间上午11:00：英国央行行长安德鲁·贝利发表讲话 本文作者为 investinglive.com 的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Bleak Friday for Asian equites, Korea down 8%
items.summary: Tokyo shares down ~4%, South Korea down ~6%. Fingers pointing at OpenAI IPO delay UBS targets $5,200/oz gold , sees current dip as buying opportunity Tokyo core-core CPI hits 1.9% as Middle East price pressures spread beyond energy PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8166 (vs. estimate at 6.8015) Iraq OPEC exit threat puts $50 oil in play as Gulf cartel frays Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) Governor Michele Bullock will be speaking over the weekend KOSPI in focus as Samsung plans 648 bln domestic push and South Korea caps fuel prices Japan - Tokyo headline CPI 1.7% y/y (expected 1.7%, prior 1.4%). Core 1.6% (1.6%, 1.3%) Goolsbee flags labour market and warns Fed credibility at risk from bad guidance Polestar shut out of US market from 2027 as China tech ban claims Geely-linked brand BOJ front-loading now in play as AI demand and inflation risks build WSJ: Polymarket paid creators to fake trades and wins in covert US social media campaign ICYMI: Iran fires on cargo ship in Hormuz strait, UN halts evacuation as oil price bounce ICYMI: NY Fed Williams delays 2% inflation target to 2028, sees policy well positioned New York Times says 'OpenAI leans toward waiting until next year for IPO' Summary: Asian equities are getting hammered after the New York Times reported OpenAI is leaning toward delaying its IPO until 2026, triggering a broad tech selloff across the region The Nikkei is down around 4.5% with SoftBank falling 12% or more, as CEO Masayoshi Son's heavily publicised bet on OpenAI comes under pressure The KOSPI dropped as much as 8%, prompting Korea Exchange to activate a sidecar on KOSPI 200 futures and then call a further 20-minute trading halt after the index extended losses Hong Kong's Hang Seng and Shanghai Composite are each down more than 2% on heavy tech selling US index futures are being pulled lower, with the Emini S&P 500 off 0.7% and the Emini Nasdaq 100 down 1.5% Oil is higher after Thursday reports of a cargo ship struck by a projectile off Oman, though Saudi Aramco's resumption of crude loading at Ras Tanura after a near four-month halt is a countervailing supply signal The US dollar is modestly stronger across the board; AUD and NZD are bearing the brunt of the risk-off move; gold and silver are lower Chicago Fed's Goolsbee repeated that core inflation remains too high and said services inflation is a little more disturbing Asian equity markets are in full retreat on Friday, with a late New York Times report that OpenAI is leaning toward pushing its IPO to next year landing like a grenade across a region already on edge from geopolitical uncertainty and elevated valuations. The Nikkei is bearing some of the sharpest pain, down around 4.5% and dragged lower by SoftBank, which has fallen 12% or more as markets reassess the timeline and terms of a listing that CEO Masayoshi Son had placed at the centre of his AI investment thesis. The OpenAI delay story is doing real damage to any stock with a direct line to that narrative. South Korea is worse. The KOSPI dropped around 6% at one stage before extending to an 8% decline, triggering a Korea Exchange sidecar on KOSPI 200 futures after they fell 5%, followed by a further 20-minute circuit breaker trading halt as the index kept sliding. It is, by any measure, a rout. Hong Kong's Hang Seng and the Shanghai Composite are each down more than 2%, with heavy tech selling the common thread. US futures are not being spared. The Emini S&P 500 is down 0.7% and the Emini Nasdaq 100 is off 1.5%, with the tech rotation that began on Wall Street resuming with force through the Asian session. In oil, prices are higher after reports of a cargo ship struck by a projectile off Oman, keeping Hormuz anxiety alive. Saudi Aramco's resumption of crude loading at Ras Tanura after a near four-month halt is the constructive counterpoint, with two VLCCs seen loading and a third waiting nearby. The dollar is edging higher across the majors, with the AUD and NZD taking the hardest hit in FX as commodity prices and risk appetite slide together. Gold and silver are lower. Fed's Goolsbee, speaking earlier in the US session, said core inflation is still too high, trending the wrong way, and described services inflation as a little more disturbing. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 东京股市下跌约4%，韩国股市下跌约6%。市场将矛头指向OpenAI推迟IPO；瑞银将黄金目标价定为每盎司5,200美元，认为当前回调是买入良机；随着中东价格压力蔓延至能源领域之外，东京核心-核心CPI升至1.9%；中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8166（市场预期为6.8015） 伊拉克威胁退出欧佩克，油价或跌至50美元，海湾产油国联盟裂痕加深 澳大利亚储备银行（RBA）行长米歇尔·布洛克将于本周末发表讲话 韩国综合股价指数（KOSPI）备受关注，三星计划投入6480亿韩元推动国内业务，韩国政府同时对燃油价格设限 日本——东京整体CPI同比1.7%（预期1.7%，前值1.4%）。 核心通胀率1.6%（1.6%，1.3%） 古尔斯比关注劳动力市场，警告称美联储的公信力可能因不当指引而受损 据称因中国科技禁令，极星（Polestar）自2027年起将被排除在美国市场之外，该品牌与吉利有关 随着AI需求和通胀风险加剧，日本央行已开始实施“前置性宽松” 《华尔街日报》： Polymarket在秘密的美国社交媒体宣传活动中付钱给创作者伪造交易和获利 值得关注：伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡向货轮开火，油价反弹之际联合国暂停撤离行动 值得关注：纽约联储威廉姆斯将2%通胀目标推迟至2028年，认为政策定位恰当 《纽约时报》称“OpenAI倾向于等到明年再进行IPO” 摘要： 《纽约时报》报道称OpenAI倾向于将IPO推迟至2026年，引发亚洲股市重挫，该地区科技股普遍遭遇抛售。日经指数下跌约4.5%，软银股价跌幅超过12%， 因首席执行官孙正义广受关注的对OpenAI押注面临压力。韩国KOSPI指数一度下跌8%，促使韩国交易所对KOSPI 200期货启动“侧车”机制，随后因指数跌幅扩大，又宣布暂停交易20分钟。受科技股抛售潮影响，香港恒生指数和上证综合指数均下跌逾2%。美国 股指期货走低，Emini标普500指数期货下跌0.7%，Emini纳斯达克100指数期货下跌1.5%。周四有报道称一艘货轮在阿曼近海遭弹体击中，受此影响油价上涨；不过，沙特阿美在停运近四个月后于拉斯塔努拉恢复原油装船，这一举措释放出供应增加的信号，对油价上涨形成对冲。美元指数全面小幅走强； 澳元和新西兰元在避险情绪蔓延中首当其冲；黄金和白银价格走低。芝加哥联储的古尔斯比重申核心通胀仍处于过高水平，并表示服务业通胀情况更令人担忧。周五亚洲股市全面回落，《纽约时报》晚间报道称OpenAI倾向于将IPO推迟至明年，这一消息犹如一颗手榴弹，在因地缘政治不确定性和估值过高而本已如坐针毡的亚洲市场引发剧烈震动。日经指数跌幅最为剧烈，下跌约4.5%，其中软银拖累大盘走低，其股价下跌12%以上，因为市场正在重新评估软银的上市时间表和条款——软银CEO孙正义曾将此作为其人工智能投资论点的核心。OpenAI推迟上市的消息正对任何与该叙事直接相关的股票造成实质性损害。 韩国的情况更糟。韩国KOSPI指数一度下跌约6%，随后跌幅扩大至8%，导致韩国交易所对KOSPI 200期货触发了“侧车”机制——该期货下跌5%后，因指数持续下挫，市场又进入为期20分钟的熔断停盘。无论从何种标准来看，这都是一场大溃败。香港恒生指数和上证综合指数均下跌超过2%，科技股遭抛售是两地市场的共同特征。美国期货也未能幸免。Emini标普500指数期货下跌0.7%，Emini纳斯达克100指数期货下跌1.5%，华尔街此前开始的科技股轮动在亚洲交易时段强势延续。原油方面，有报道称一艘货轮在阿曼近海遭弹丸击中，使霍尔木兹海峡的紧张局势持续发酵，油价因此走高。沙特阿美在停运近四个月后，于拉斯塔努拉重启原油装船作业，成为利好因素，目前有两艘超大型油轮（VLCC）正在装货，第三艘则在附近等待。美元兑主要货币小幅走高，随着大宗商品价格与风险偏好同步下滑，澳元和新西兰元在外汇市场遭受重创。黄金和白银价格走低。 美联储的古尔斯比在美股交易时段早些时候表示，核心通胀率仍然过高，且走势不妙，并称服务业通胀情况更令人担忧。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹。
items.summaryEn: Tokyo shares down ~4%, South Korea down ~6%. Fingers pointing at OpenAI IPO delay UBS targets $5,200/oz gold , sees current dip as buying opportunity Tokyo core-core CPI hits 1.9% as Middle East price pressures spread beyond energy PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8166 (vs. estimate at 6.8015) Iraq OPEC exit threat puts $50 oil in play as Gulf cartel frays Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) Governor Michele Bullock will be speaking over the weekend KOSPI in focus as Samsung plans 648 bln domestic push and South Korea caps fuel prices Japan - Tokyo headline CPI 1.7% y/y (expected 1.7%, prior 1.4%). Core 1.6% (1.6%, 1.3%) Goolsbee flags labour market and warns Fed credibility at risk from bad guidance Polestar shut out of US market from 2027 as China tech ban claims Geely-linked brand BOJ front-loading now in play as AI demand and inflation risks build WSJ: Polymarket paid creators to fake trades and wins in covert US social media campaign ICYMI: Iran fires on cargo ship in Hormuz strait, UN halts evacuation as oil price bounce ICYMI: NY Fed Williams delays 2% inflation target to 2028, sees policy well positioned New York Times says 'OpenAI leans toward waiting until next year for IPO' Summary: Asian equities are getting hammered after the New York Times reported OpenAI is leaning toward delaying its IPO until 2026, triggering a broad tech selloff across the region The Nikkei is down around 4.5% with SoftBank falling 12% or more, as CEO Masayoshi Son's heavily publicised bet on OpenAI comes under pressure The KOSPI dropped as much as 8%, prompting Korea Exchange to activate a sidecar on KOSPI 200 futures and then call a further 20-minute trading halt after the index extended losses Hong Kong's Hang Seng and Shanghai Composite are each down more than 2% on heavy tech selling US index futures are being pulled lower, with the Emini S&P 500 off 0.7% and the Emini Nasdaq 100 down 1.5% Oil is higher after Thursday reports of a cargo ship struck by a projectile off Oman, though Saudi Aramco's resumption of crude loading at Ras Tanura after a near four-month halt is a countervailing supply signal The US dollar is modestly stronger across the board; AUD and NZD are bearing the brunt of the risk-off move; gold and silver are lower Chicago Fed's Goolsbee repeated that core inflation remains too high and said services inflation is a little more disturbing Asian equity markets are in full retreat on Friday, with a late New York Times report that OpenAI is leaning toward pushing its IPO to next year landing like a grenade across a region already on edge from geopolitical uncertainty and elevated valuations. The Nikkei is bearing some of the sharpest pain, down around 4.5% and dragged lower by SoftBank, which has fallen 12% or more as markets reassess the timeline and terms of a listing that CEO Masayoshi Son had placed at the centre of his AI investment thesis. The OpenAI delay story is doing real damage to any stock with a direct line to that narrative. South Korea is worse. The KOSPI dropped around 6% at one stage before extending to an 8% decline, triggering a Korea Exchange sidecar on KOSPI 200 futures after they fell 5%, followed by a further 20-minute circuit breaker trading halt as the index kept sliding. It is, by any measure, a rout. Hong Kong's Hang Seng and the Shanghai Composite are each down more than 2%, with heavy tech selling the common thread. US futures are not being spared. The Emini S&P 500 is down 0.7% and the Emini Nasdaq 100 is off 1.5%, with the tech rotation that began on Wall Street resuming with force through the Asian session. In oil, prices are higher after reports of a cargo ship struck by a projectile off Oman, keeping Hormuz anxiety alive. Saudi Aramco's resumption of crude loading at Ras Tanura after a near four-month halt is the constructive counterpoint, with two VLCCs seen loading and a third waiting nearby. The dollar is edging higher across the majors, with the AUD and NZD taking the hardest hit in FX as commodity prices and risk appetite slide together. Gold and silver are lower. Fed's Goolsbee, speaking earlier in the US session, said core inflation is still too high, trending the wrong way, and described services inflation as a little more disturbing. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive European markets wrap: Oil drops further, stocks down with eyes on big tech
items.summary: Headlines: US futures marked lower after tumultuous Wall Street session yesterday EUR/USD is retesting a key zone amid US dollar weakness on peak hawkish repricing Iran says Strait of Hormuz shipping will be governed by terms of memorandum with Oman There's still mud in the waters on how things are playing out with the Strait of Hormuz How have interest rate expectations changed after this week's events? ECB survey shows inflation expectations dropping markedly and growth outlook improving Mild dollar buying expected this month-end - Credit Agricole Markets: S&P 500 futures down 0.5%, Nasdaq futures down 1.2% European stocks drop, DAX down 1.2% and CAC 40 down 0.8% WTI crude down 3% to $69.70 EUR leads, AUD lags on the day US 10-year yields down 1 bps to 4.38% Gold up 0.6% to $4,050 It was a more tentative session as markets are holding more mixed. While traders and investor remain calm on the US-Iran situation as reflected in oil prices, there is some nervousness and anxiety in equities after the AI push and pull yesterday. Big tech is in focus after the selloff yesterday and that's leading to a more sluggish mood with S&P 500 futures down 0.5% and Nasdaq futures down 1.2% on the day. All eyes will be on how Wall Street takes to that later today, with the reaction being one that could spill over to broader markets. As we await further US-Iran developments, oil prices are down with WTI crude lower by 3% to $69.70. Technical talks on Iran's nuclear/uranium will only begin next week but for now, traders are sensing some calm despite mixed signals from the Strait of Hormuz. In other markets, the dollar is down slightly after the more solid gains this week. EUR/USD is back up by 0.3% to 1.1400 while USD/JPY is still just keeping thereabouts near the 2024 highs, down 0.1% today to 161.60 levels. Meanwhile, precious metals are also seeing a slight bounce towards the final stretch of the week. Gold is up 0.6% to $4,050 with silver up 0.6% to $58.20 on the day. There's not much else to it as we also near month-end and quarter-end, with rebalancing flows perhaps a consideration before the weekend and/or early next week into the London fix. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 头条：受昨日华尔街震荡交易日影响，美股期货低开 受美元走软及鹰派预期回调至峰值影响，欧元兑美元正重新测试关键区间 伊朗表示，霍尔木兹海峡的航运将遵循与阿曼签署的备忘录条款 霍尔木兹海峡局势的发展仍不明朗 本周事件后，利率预期发生了哪些变化？欧洲央行调查显示通胀预期显著下降，经济增长前景改善 法国农业信贷银行预计本月底将出现温和的美元买盘 市场：标普500指数期货下跌0.5%，纳斯达克指数期货下跌1.2% 欧洲股市下跌，DAX指数下跌1.2%，CAC 40指数下跌0.8% WTI原油下跌3%至69.70美元 欧元领跌， 澳元当日表现落后 美国10年期国债收益率下跌1个基点至4.38% 黄金上涨0.6%至4,050美元 由于市场情绪较为复杂，今日交易走势较为谨慎。尽管交易员和投资者对美伊局势保持冷静——这从油价走势中可见一斑——但在昨日人工智能板块的震荡行情之后，股市中仍存在一些紧张和焦虑情绪。 在昨日抛售潮之后，科技巨头股成为市场焦点，这导致市场情绪趋于低迷，标普500指数期货当日下跌0.5%，纳斯达克指数期货下跌1.2%。市场将密切关注华尔街今日稍晚对此作何反应，因为这种反应可能会波及更广泛的市场。在等待美伊局势进一步发展之际，油价出现下跌，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）下跌3%，至69.70美元。关于伊朗核问题及铀问题的技术性谈判要到下周才会开始，但目前尽管霍尔木兹海峡传来的信号喜忧参半，交易员们仍感到市场有所平静。在其他市场方面，美元在本周录得较为稳健的涨幅后，今日小幅走低。欧元兑美元回升0.3%至1.1400，而美元兑日元仍徘徊在2024年高点附近，今日下跌0.1%至161.60水平。与此同时，贵金属在本周收官阶段也出现小幅反弹。黄金上涨0.6%至4,050美元，白银当日上涨0.6%至58.20美元。随着月末和季度末临近，市场动态较为平淡，周末前及/或下周初伦敦定盘价公布前，资产再平衡资金流或许值得关注。本文由investinglive.com的Justin Low撰写。
